change_summary: Decider applied 5 c3 patches (commit c19a1e7, ISS-1262 through ISS-1266): triplication fix + Domain 8 misattribution + 14-month window argument + rate/cumulative equivalence clarification + near-duplicate Domain 8 sentence removal. Re-reviewing the patched detail_evidence to verify accuracy and look for new defects introduced.
our_argument_summary: All five c3 findings have been correctly addressed. The WIN-042 cross-reference sentence now appears once (verified via indexOf count = 1). The Domain 8 misattribution is fixed: the prose now distinguishes the static W011 confirmation (~30 nT) from the live Domain 8 panel (77.0 nT/yr PRED=OBS identity), correctly framing Domain 8 as reinforcing the SAA-only geographic limitation rather than as the threshold check. The 14-month window kicker (25.7 nT/yr below 28 nT/yr threshold) is present and the math checks out (30 * 12 / 14 = 25.71). The rate/cumulative equivalence sentence now bridges WIN-037 and WIN-042 explicitly. The Misleading verdict still holds. ONE NEW PROSE DEFECT slipped through: two consecutive sentences both contain 'batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date' — a near-duplication that reads as sloppy prose to any defender screenshotting the page.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-037 maps to PROS-007 and to the static W011 entry. The confirmation text is hardcoded in the WINs file: "NOAA WMM2025 / INTERMAGNET confirms SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025. Exceeds threshold." There is no monitor.py domain that actively recomputes the 28 nT / 12-month threshold against live data. Domain 8 in raw-text/08-live-power.txt is labeled "SAA Decay Rate" and reports Predicted 77.0 nT/yr = Observed 77.0 nT/yr (identity check) with 20% tolerance — a separate panel measuring a different number against a different method. The c4 patched prose correctly distinguishes these two artifacts.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. The confirmation ("~30 nT dropped") is static text in raw-text/02-wins.txt; the Domain 8 live panel is a separate PRED=OBS identity at 77.0 nT/yr. Neither is a live test of the 28 nT / 12-month threshold.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The word "global" in PROS-007 is unbacked by any global computation. If SAA region drops >=28 nT in any 12-month window the dome claims confirmation. The 14-month window in the confirmation text (vs 12 months in the prediction) creates a quiet inflation: ~30 nT over 14 months annualizes to 25.7 nT/yr, BELOW the threshold. The dome counts the cumulative number anyway.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None remaining after c3 patches. Domain 8 attribution is now correctly framed as the live panel reinforcing SAA-only geography, separate from the static W011 confirmation that backs the 28 nT claim. Verified Domain 8 raw text says "Predicted: 77.0 nT/yr | Observed: 77.0 nT/yr | Tolerance: 20.0% | Source: INTERMAGNET TTB annual means" — matches our prose.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: The W011 confirmation (~30 nT) is static text in raw-text/02-wins.txt line 45 and WIN list line 125. There is no live recomputation path. The separate Domain 8 "SAA Decay Rate" panel is also hardcoded (77.0 = 77.0 PRED=OBS identity).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA regional decay of 20-35 nT/yr is tracked continuously by NOAA WMM, ESA Swarm, INTERMAGNET, and IGRF. Mechanism (reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary) is mainstream geomagnetism. The dome relabels as "aetheric field decay" but predicts the same numerical value from the same INTERMAGNET data.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PROS-007 registered 2026-03-06, confirmed by 2026-03-12 (6-day turnaround). WMM2025 released December 2024; INTERMAGNET TTB data continuously public. Batch of five (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on 2026-03-06 — same-day scan of available data. SAA has declined at 20-35 nT/yr for over a decade, so >=28 nT / 12-month threshold was effectively pre-satisfied at registration.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: raw-text/05-model.txt line 42 lists "Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year" as a core model parameter. No formula connects disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any dome geometry to 28 nT/yr. Value is observationally adopted, not geometrically derived.

holes_found.description: Near-duplicate 'batch of five predictions' phrase in two consecutive sentences. detail_evidence at positions ~2253 and ~2355 contains: (1) 'This pattern (register prediction → confirm with already-public data within days) recurs across the batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date.' followed immediately by (2) 'It was part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction.' Two consecutive sentences both ending with the identical phrase 'batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date' will read as sloppy prose. A defender can screenshot this and dismiss the entire detail block.
holes_found.recommendation: Collapse the two sentences into one: 'This pattern (register → confirm with already-public data within days) recurs across the batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on 2026-03-06, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction.' This preserves the 'recurs across the batch' analytical point AND the 'suggesting systematic data mining' kicker without restating the WIN-035-039 list twice.

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence sentence 'WIN-037's confirmation is a static W011 entry (~30 nT over 12–14 months, no live recomputation).' is parenthetically using '12–14 months' to gloss the time-window mismatch we just established two sentences earlier. A defender could read '12–14 months' as us conceding the prediction window is flexible. Recommend tightening to '~30 nT over the dome's 14-month confirmation window' to make the inconsistency the dome's, not ours.
holes_found.recommendation: Rephrase to: "WIN-037's confirmation is a static W011 entry (~30 nT over the dome's 14-month reporting window, no live recomputation)." — this re-attributes the 14-month window to the dome rather than appearing to fudge it ourselves.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence states 'WIN-042 makes the mathematically equivalent claim framed as an annualized rate (≥28 nT/yr) rather than a 12-month cumulative threshold.' This is fine, but it would be even tighter to add the implication: 'so a single SAA observation can be counted twice — once as a 12-month cumulative WIN, once as an annualized-rate WIN.' This makes the manipulation explicit rather than implied.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: append '— meaning a single SAA observation can be counted twice (once cumulative, once as a rate)' to the WIN-042 cross-reference sentence. Not blocking; current version is adequate.

stronger_arguments.description: The c3 stronger_argument about IGRF-13 secular variation maps showing positive intensification across much of Asia and the Pacific is still untaken. A 'global' decay claim is mathematically incompatible with positive surface intensification over ~30-40% of Earth's surface. This is the strongest available rebuttal to the regional-vs-global bait-and-switch and remains uncited.
sources: IGRF-13 / IGRF-14 secular variation map (Alken et al., Earth Planets and Space, 2021)

stronger_arguments.description: detail_verdict_text could leverage the '99% of the decay had already accumulated by registration date' framing more punchily. Current text says 'By registration, ~99% of the 12-month decay had already occurred; the observation was essentially complete.' We could add: 'Predicting 99%-completed events is retrodiction, full stop — the timestamp records when we wrote it down, not when it was uncertain.'
sources: PROS-007 registration date 2026-03-06 vs claimed window March 2025-March 2026

stronger_arguments.description: We do not preempt the dome defender's strongest counter to the parameter-as-prediction argument: 'Newton's G is a parameter and yet measuring G doesn't make Newtonian gravity circular.' The c3 advocate_mode flagged this and recommended adding a paragraph about discriminating predictions vs parameter-as-prediction tautology. The patched prose did not add this rebuttal. The disanalogy (Newton's G enables hundreds of cross-checks; the dome's 28 nT/yr enables exactly one prediction — that 28 nT/yr continues) is still missing.

kernel_of_truth.description: The SAA IS decaying faster than the global average. The ~28-30 nT/yr figure at Tsumeb is real and well-measured. The dome correctly identifies this as a notable geomagnetic phenomenon. The new 14-month observation window adds a small but real datum to that established trend.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Standard geomagnetism attributes SAA decay to reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (MHD plus paleomagnetic reconstructions). The dome offers no alternative physical mechanism, no derivation from dome geometry, and no numerical prediction that differs from a WMM2025 extrapolation. Accurately observing a known phenomenon that standard physics already predicts is a data lookup, not a model confirmation. The 14-month window kicker reinforces this: even on the dome's own preferred station, the implied annualized rate (25.7 nT/yr) does not meet the dome's own threshold (28 nT/yr) — only the cumulative number does, and only because the dome quietly used a 14-month window.

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am a dome defender steelmanning the post-patch WIN-037. Your moderate hole is wordsmithing — two sentences ending in the same phrase is not a scientific defect, it is a copyedit issue. The substantive arguments (regional-vs-global, parameter-as-prediction, batch registration, 14-month window) are now consolidated and strong. On 14-month window: my counter is that 30 nT cumulative IS observed, regardless of whether you express it as a rate. The prediction was a 12-month cumulative threshold (>=28 nT in 12 months); the SAA dropped >=28 nT in <14 months at the latest, which means it dropped >=28 nT in 12 months at the SAA's actual decay rate. Your annualization (25.7 nT/yr) is a derived number that does NOT contradict the dome's specific cumulative claim. On parameter-as-prediction: see Newton's G — I will repeat this until you address it. On 'static W011 confirmation': you concede the dome has a live monitoring panel (Domain 8) that does check this — that you find it tolerance-gamed is your opinion, not a methodological violation.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Address the 'cumulative threshold met = prediction confirmed' counter explicitly. The dome's prediction language is '>=28 nT in 12 months', which is technically a threshold-comparison test. The defender will say: even if the rate annualizes to 25.7, the cumulative is still 30 (or thereabouts) over the actual measurement window, and 30 > 28. Our rebuttal: the 12-month wording is load-bearing — a cumulative test conducted over a longer window (14 months) is not a 12-month test. The dome cannot stretch the window AND keep the cumulative threshold; that is moving the goalposts. Compact text to add: 'A cumulative threshold (>=28 nT in 12 months) is not satisfied by a measurement spanning 14 months. The dome cannot stretch the window without re-stating the threshold; doing both silently is goalpost-shifting.' Also: revisit the c3 stronger_arguments[1] about Newton's G — still not in the prose. Adding a single Newton's-G-disanalogy sentence would close the most-cited dome-defender counter.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No contradictions across the batch. WIN-035 (SAA African cell <21,795 nT), WIN-036 (NP deviation), WIN-038 (Schumann), WIN-039 (Lunar phase magnetic) all share the 2026-03-06 batch-registration date and the 'register prediction then confirm with already-public data within days' pattern. The WIN-037 detail_evidence cross-references this batch consistently (with the duplication noted above). WIN-042 is now explicitly bridged via the rate/cumulative equivalence sentence — the 'two labels for one observation' argument is airtight.

claims_checked: WIN-042 sentence triplication count after patch (should be 1)
Domain 8 sentence count after patch (should be 1)
Domain 8 raw text confirms 77.0 = 77.0 nT/yr at INTERMAGNET TTB (Tsumeb) — verifying our framing
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) indexOf 'mathematically equivalent claim' = 1 ✓ (was 3 in c3, fixed). (2) indexOf 'Domain 8' = 1 ✓ (was 2 in c3, fixed). (3) 30 * 12 / 14 = 25.7142... ≈ 25.7 ✓ (matches our prose). (4) 0.05 * 30000 / 100 = 15.0 nT/yr ✓ (matches our prose). (5) raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 228-230 confirm 'Domain: SAA Decay Rate, Predicted: 77.0 nT/yr | Observed: 77.0 nT/yr | Source: INTERMAGNET TTB annual means' — TTB = Tsumeb, our prose accurate. NEW DUPLICATION FOUND: 'batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date' appears twice (positions 2253 and 2355) in two consecutive sentences.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None substantive. The 25.7 nT/yr sub-finding is mathematically correct and has been correctly written into the prose. The new prose defect is the 'batch of five predictions' duplication — same defect family as the c3 triplication (consecutive sentences with overlapping content), suggesting a recurring patch-application weakness in the decider's text-merge logic when c3 recommendations stack near existing prose. Worth a tinker-level look at the patch pipeline.

deeper_analysis: The decider's c3 patch application was generally clean: all five identified holes have been correctly addressed, the math is verified, and the new prose flows reasonably. The Misleading verdict is even more strongly supported now that the 14-month window kicker exposes a second quiet manipulation (the dome stretches the window AND keeps the cumulative threshold). One new prose defect slipped in — the 'batch of five predictions' duplication — which is the same defect FAMILY as the c3 triplication (consecutive sentences with near-identical content), suggesting the patch-merge logic does not check for adjacent sentence redundancy. This is a moderate prose hole worth fixing in c5; it is NOT a verdict-changing problem. Two stronger arguments remain unimplemented from c3: (a) IGRF secular-variation map showing positive intensification across ~30-40% of Earth — would close the regional-vs-global argument completely; (b) Newton's G disanalogy paragraph preempting the parameter-as-prediction defense. Both are still recommended for c5.
